action.reasoning: Observation: v8 heuristic very weak (avg safe_steps=10, max=43/500, 0 success, found_rate=0, avg_dist~6m, occasional collisions). Test confirms config thresholds (500 steps in [1-2)m at |angle|<0.2618rad/15deg). Some episodes reach human (min_dist=0.62, detector used) but lose track fast due to random oracle movement. Analysis: overly complex OA/gains in v8 hurt seeking; reactive P-control insufficient without planning (literature SPF heuristic gets ~0.5 follow-rate). Root cause: no map-aware pathing to dynamic target leads to obstacles/stuck/lost track. Hybrid SPF (for pursuit to ~1.5m standoff using world human pos from sim.get_agent_state(0)+other_agent_gps) + reactive facing/distance/speed-match when close should improve substantially. Decision: implement v9 hybrid in full test_full_episode.py (explore SPF action ints, human_pos, count all diagnostics) + complete predict.py (same policy, full JSON contract with per_episode, means, safe_follow_steps count using exact config angle, output dir creation) + train.py (version note). Run test_full_episode.py to validate (expect higher safe_steps), then submit_eval.
